title: About
text: Hi I am Lucas! Welcome to my blog about intersting sports things, mostly biased to my favorite teams.

title: Nebraska had a program first in March Madness
text:
Code
library(tidyverse)


── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.6
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.1     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors


Nebraska’s March Madness run was one for the ages. The Husker’s secured their first March Madness win in program history over Troy. They came into the tournament being the only power 4 team to have never won a tournament game


Code
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = disp, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point()

title: From Champions of Europe to Constant Coaching Carousells: What Happened to Chelsea FC?
text:
Code
library(tidyverse)


── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.6
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.1     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──

✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()

Code
library(patchwork)
library(ggbump)
library(ggrepel)
library(gt)
library(ggalt)
library(ggbeeswarm)
CLSeason <- read_csv("MajorProject/Pl2020_21.csv")


Rows: 20 Columns: 28
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr  (2): Season, Squad
dbl (26): Rank, MP, W, D, L, GF, GA, GD, Pts, Pts_per_MP, Num_Players, Avg_A...

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Rows: 20 Columns: 28
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr  (2): Season, Squad
dbl (26): Rank, MP, W, D, L, GF, GA, GD, Pts, Pts_per_MP, Num_Players, Avg_A...

ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.


Code
ChelseaRank <- read.csv("MajorProject/Chelsea_rank_2020_26.csv")


Chelsea FC had finally climbed the mountain again in 2021. WIth a 1-0 win over Manchester City on a faithful night in Wembly, the Pride of London retook their throne as Champions of Europe. With a solid core, a great coach in Tomas Tuchel, and the full support of the ownership, they seemed to be untouchable. The thing that brought them down… the Rusian Invasion of Ukraine.
You might be wondering how that could possibly bring an English football team from the top of the mountain to a staggering 12th place the next season. After the invasion started in 2022, the English FA forced Russian billionare Ruben Abromivich, the owner of Chelsea to sell to Clearlake Captial and Todd Bohley, a group that would soon be known as Blueco.
Todd and Clearlake had an idea. This idea was a project to build a dynasty for years to come. They purchased about every player under the sun, however, they were all under age 25. Managers found this contrevercial due to lack of experience, however it was the decision of Blueco at the end of the day.
As we enter year 4 of the Chelsea project, are on their 6th manager, billions spent in transfer fees, and no leauge titles or champions leauges to show for it.
Lets dive into some of the numbers from their championship winning year versus recent years to identify where the glaring holes are to see if money can patch them starting with goal differential:


Code
CLDumbell <- ggplot() + 
  geom_segment(
    data=CLSeason, 
    aes(
      y=Squad, yend=Squad,
      x=GF, xend=GA),
    color="grey",
    linewidth = 1.5
  )  + 
  geom_point(
    data=CLSeason,
    aes(
      y=Squad,
      x=GF,
      color="GF"
    ),
    size=2
  ) + 
  geom_point(
    data=CLSeason,
    aes(
      y=Squad,
      x=GA,
      color="GA"
    ),
    size=2
  ) + 
  scale_color_manual(name="Goals", values=c("red", "green")) +
  labs(
    title = "Goals For and Against in Chelsea's 
    Champions Leauge Winning Season",
    subtitle = "This is the easiest baseline to see what happened to the team. 
    Did they stop scoring? Did they let in more goals? Lets find out:",
    y = "",
    x = "Goals For/ Goals Against CL Season",
    caption="Source: FBRef| By Lucas Krapfl"
  )+
  theme_minimal()+ 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
    )

Dumbell23 <- ggplot() + 
  geom_segment(
    data=Season2023, 
    aes(
      y=Squad, yend=Squad,
      x=GF, xend=GA),
    color="grey",
    linewidth = 1.5
  )  + 
  geom_point(
    data=Season2023,
    aes(
      y=Squad,
      x=GF,
      color="GF"
    ),
    size=2
  ) + 
  geom_point(
    data=Season2023,
    aes(
      y=Squad,
      x=GA,
      color="GA"
    ),
    size=2
  ) + 
  scale_color_manual(name="Goals", values=c("red", "green")) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    subtitle = "",
    y = "",
    x = "Goals For/ Goals Against 22-23",
    caption="Source: FBRef | By Lucas Krapfl"
  )+
  theme_minimal()+ 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
    )
CLDumbell + Dumbell23









Right off the bat we can see that the goal differential took a massive hit when key players started to leave, specifically their defensive anchor Antonio Rudiger. This poor performance would also lead to the departure of basically the entire offense in Kai Havertz, Mason Mount, Christian Pulisic, Mateo Kovacic, and more. Lets take another look at just how bad Chelsea’s squad got under Blueco.


Code
Chelsea23 <- Season2023 |> filter(Squad == "Chelsea")
ChelseaCL<- CLSeason |> filter(Squad == "Chelsea") 
Chelsea24<- Season2024 |> filter(Squad == "Chelsea") 
CLBubble<- ggplot() + 
  geom_point(
    data=CLSeason, 
    aes(x=GF, y=GA, size=GD), 
    color="grey", 
    alpha=.3) + 
  geom_point(
    data=ChelseaCL, 
    aes(x=GF, y=GA, size=GD), 
    color="blue") +
labs(
    title = "How much worse did Chelsea's Goal 
    Difference Get Under Blueco?",
    subtitle = "The bubble chart will show us if they moved along either axis or even 
switched quadrants during Blueco's first year:",
    y = "Goals Against",
    x = "Goals For CL",
   caption="Source: FBRef | By Lucas Krapfl" ) +
   theme_minimal()+ 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold")
    ) +
  geom_text_repel(data=ChelseaCL, aes(x=GF, y=GA, label=Squad)) +
  annotate("rect", fill = "blue", alpha = 0.1, 
        xmin = 50, xmax = Inf,
        ymin = 30, ymax = 40) +
  geom_text(aes(x=65, y=41, label="Great Teams"), size=3) 
  
Bubble2023<-ggplot() + 
  labs(
    y = "Goals Against",
    x = "Goals For 22/23",
   caption="Source: FBRef | By Lucas Krapfl" )+
   theme_minimal()+ 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold")
    ) +
  geom_point(
    data=Season2023, 
    aes(x=GF, y=GA, size=GD), 
    color="grey", 
    alpha=.3) + 
  geom_point(
    data=Chelsea23, 
    aes(x=GF, y=GA, size=GD), 
    color="blue") +
  geom_text_repel(data=Chelsea23, aes(x=GF, y=GA, label=Squad)) +
  annotate("rect", fill = "blue", alpha = 0.1, 
        xmin = 60, xmax = Inf,
        ymin = 30, ymax = 40) +
  geom_text(aes(x=82, y=37, label="Great Teams"), size=3) +
labs(
    y = "Goals Against",
    x = "Goals For 23/24",
   caption="Source: FBRef | By Lucas Krapfl" )+
   theme_minimal()+ 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot")
CLBubble + Bubble2023









This is shows that even after they spent a billion pounds on all the flashiest names to score all the cool highlight goals, you cannot win championships without a key defense. This project was proposed under the guise of development of young players, however when you fire the best developmental coach in the land and replace with 5 more yes men, clearly it does not work. Lets see if in the first season after Tuchel was fired if the coaching or the veteran leadership failed the project:


Code
CLTable <- CLSeason |>
  select(Squad, Rank, Avg_Age, GD) |>
  arrange((Rank))

Table2023 <- Season2023 |>
  select(Squad, Rank, Avg_Age, GD) |>
  arrange((Rank))

CLTable |>
  gt() |> 
  cols_label(
    Rank = "Final Rank",
    Avg_Age = "Avg. Player Age",
    GD= "Goal Differential"
  ) |>
  tab_header(
    title = "Did the Decrease in Veterans Contribute to the Downfall of Chelsea?",
    subtitle = "When most of the veteran leaders left the club, with young players joining, how did the rankings change:"
  ) |> tab_style(
    style = cell_text(color = "black", weight = "bold", align = "left"),
    locations = cells_title("title")
  ) |> tab_style(
    style = cell_text(color = "black", align = "left"),
    locations = cells_title("subtitle")
  ) |>
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md("Source: FbRef | By Lucas Krapfl")
  ) |>
  tab_style(
     locations = cells_column_labels(columns = everything()),
     style = list(
       cell_borders(sides = "bottom", weight = px(3)),
       cell_text(weight = "bold", size=12)
     )
   ) |>
  opt_row_striping() |> 
  opt_table_lines("none") |>
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "blue"),
      cell_text(color = "white")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = Squad == "Chelsea")
  )

This highlights the biggest problem with Blueco that fans have been yelling about for years… A YES MAN IS NOT A GOOD MANAGER. When you sack the best manager you have had it will not matter if you have veterans or not, you will lose. Lets take a look at what they did the year after their spending spree:


Code
CLBeeswarm <- ggplot() + 
  geom_jitter(
    data=CLSeason, 
    aes(x=Avg_Age, y=Pts), color="grey") + 
  geom_jitter(
    data=ChelseaCL, 
    aes(x=Avg_Age, y=Pts), color="Blue") + 
  geom_text_repel(
    data=ChelseaCL, 
    aes(x=Avg_Age, y=Pts, label=Squad)) +
  theme_minimal()+ 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
    ) +
  labs(
    title = "Trust The Process or Seek Experience?",
    subtitle = "This will show us how many points each Chelsea Squad scored in leauge play relative to their team setup. The Champions Leauge squad was built around middle-age vets while the project squad is $1 billon youngsters",
    y = "Points",
    x = "Average Age CL Season",
   caption="Source: FBRef | By Lucas Krapfl" )+
   theme_minimal()+ 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold")
    ) +
  annotate("rect", fill = "cyan", alpha = 0.1, 
        xmin = 23, xmax = Inf,
        ymin = 80, ymax = Inf) +
  geom_text(aes(x=25, y=85, label="Man City Tier"), size=3) +
  annotate("rect", fill = "green", alpha = 0.1, 
        xmin = 23, xmax = Inf,
        ymin = 64.5, ymax = 80) +
  geom_text(aes(x=24, y=75, label="Title Contenders"), size=3) +
  annotate("rect", fill = "yellow", alpha = 0.1, 
        xmin = 23, xmax = Inf,
        ymin = 60, ymax = 64.5) +
  geom_text(aes(x=24, y=63, label="UCL Race"), size=3) + 
  annotate("rect", fill = "red", alpha = 0.1, 
        xmin = 23, xmax = Inf,
        ymin = 57, ymax = 60) +
  geom_text(aes(x=24, y=59, label="Just Missed UCL"), size=3) 

Beeswarm24 <- ggplot() + 
  geom_jitter(
    data=Season2024, 
    aes(x=Avg_Age, y=Pts), color="grey") + 
  geom_jitter(
    data=Chelsea24, 
    aes(x=Avg_Age, y=Pts), color="Blue") + 
  geom_text_repel(
    data=Chelsea24, 
    aes(x=Avg_Age, y=Pts, label=Squad)) +
  theme_minimal()+ 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
    ) +
labs(
    y = "Points",
    x = "Average Age Year 2 (23/24)",
   caption="Source: FBRef | By Lucas Krapfl" )+
  theme_minimal()+ 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
    ) +
   annotate("rect", fill = "green", alpha = 0.1, 
        xmin = 23, xmax = Inf,
        ymin = 73, ymax = Inf) +
  geom_text(aes(x=27, y=85, label="Title Contenders"), size=3) +
  annotate("rect", fill = "yellow", alpha = 0.1, 
        xmin = 23, xmax = Inf,
        ymin = 65, ymax = 73) +
  geom_text(aes(x=27, y=72, label="Champions Leauge Race"), size=3) +
 annotate("rect", fill = "red", alpha = 0.1, 
        xmin = 23, xmax = Inf,
        ymin = 57, ymax = 65) +
  geom_text(aes(x=26.5, y=63, label="Just Missed UCL"), size=3) 
CLBeeswarm + Beeswarm24









While this is a vast improvement from 12th place the year before, you are talking about Chelsea FC, a team that is used to winning the biggest and best trophies, competing in the Europa Conference Leauge on WEEKDAYS. Oh and that thing I said about managers clashing with the sporting directors… after digging the team out of this hole the sporting directors fired him for asking for defenders at the end of the season!
Lets take one last step back and see where we are today with the Chelsea experiment:


Code
ggplot() + 
  geom_bump(data=ChelseaRank, aes(x=Season, y=Rank, color="Chelsea")) +
  geom_point(data=ChelseaRank, aes(x=Season, y=Rank, color="Chelsea"), size = 4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Chelsea" = "#034694"
  )) +
  geom_text(data = ChelseaRank |> filter(Season == min(Season)), aes(x = Season , y=Rank, label = Squad), size = 3, hjust = 1) +
  geom_text(data = ChelseaRank |> filter(Season == max(Season)), aes(x = Season , y=Rank, label = Squad), size = 3, hjust = 0) +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  labs(title="Premiere Leauge Final Placments since Champions Leauge Win", subtitle="Clearly the project has started to dig them out of a hole, 
however money cannot buy trophies and chemistry .", y= "Premiere Leauge Rank", x = "Season", caption="Source: FbRef | By Lucas Krapfl") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    plot.title.position = "plot")









Currently sitting in 6th place, in a horrible run of form, eliminated from the UCL, the face of your team Enzo Fernandez and Cole Palmer wanting to leave this summer, and Financial Fair Play charges looming, it is looking like the blues for the Blues might continue as long as Blueco is at the helm.

title: sportsblog
text:
